content.problemStatement:
Debug the code in the IDE to solve the problem.

The code is supposed to do the following:


Take a positive integer as input.
If it's between 1 - 7, prints the corresponding day of the week.
Else print, **Invalid input**. Check the sample test case.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer n.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the corresponding day of the week (Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday) if 1 ≤ n ≤ 7; otherwise print "Invalid input". Each answer is printed on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: 2 maps to Tuesday and 4 maps to Thursday.
8 and 0 are out of range, 7 maps to Sunday.

hidden.label: maximum boundary
hidden.expectedOutput:
Sunday
Invalid input
Invalid input
Saturday
Tuesday

hidden.label: uniform/edge case
hidden.expectedOutput:
Invalid input
Invalid input
Invalid input

hidden.label: stress/general
hidden.expectedOutput:
Wednesday
Thursday
Friday
Saturday
Sunday
Tuesday
Monday

solution.approach: The problem requires a direct mapping from numbers 1 to 7 to the names of the days of the week. For each test case we read the integer n, check whether it lies in the inclusive range [1,7] and output the corresponding string from a predefined array. If n is outside this range we output the fixed message "Invalid input". The algorithm is O(1) per test case and uses O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) total, O(1) per test case.
